metadata.composer: 'Eventide' William H. Monk, 1861 William H. Monk, 1861
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2012 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Edward Henry Bickersteth, 1872.  Music: 'Eventide' William H. Monk, 1861.  Setting: William H. Monk, 1861.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2012 Revision.

1.raw_text: Not worthy, Lord, to gather up the crumbs With trembling hand that from Thy table fall, A weary, heavy laden sinner comes To plead Thy promise and obey Thy call.

2.raw_text: I am not worthy to be thought Thy child, Nor sit the last and lowest at Thy board; Too long a wanderer and too oft beguiled; I only ask one reconciling word.

3.raw_text: One word from Thee, my Lord, one smile, one look, And I could face the cold, rough world again; And with that treasure in my heart could brook The wrath of devils and the scorn of men.

4.raw_text: And is not mercy Thy preroga- tive\- Free mercy, boundless, fathomless, divine? Me, Lord, the chief of sinners, me forgive, And Thine the greater glory, only Thine.

5.raw_text: I hear Thy voice; Thou bid'st me come and rest; I come, I kneel, I clasp Thy piercèd feet; Thou bid'st me take my place, a welcome guest Among Thy saints, and of Thy banquet eat.
